metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the commit message (containing the `rebar-ticket:` trailer) reaches `assemble_diff` — add a `commit_message` input to `assemble_diff_context`, thread it through `assemble_diff_input` schema, the `assemble_diff` YAML step, `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` inputs, `voter.py` (via `gc.get_commit` or a separate message fetch), and `shim.py`.
findings.finding: The plan says 'add trailer parsing via extract_ticket_refs' inside assemble_diff/assemble.py, but the commit message containing rebar-ticket: trailers is never part of the unified diff text and is not currently passed anywhere in the assemble_diff call chain. assemble_diff_context() accepts only (base, head, diff_text, changed_files); gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict() does not accept or forward a commit message; voter.py fetches only the patch diff (gc.get_patch / _assemble_merge_diff), never the commit body. Without a new commit_message input flowing through the assemble_diff_input schema, the YAML step, gate_dispatch inputs dict, and the voter/shim call sites, extract_ticket_refs has nothing to parse — the mechanism as described cannot fire.
findings.location: What section / assemble.py / assemble_diff_input.schema.json / code-review.yaml / gate_dispatch.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the full input pipeline for the commit message: name `produce_code_review_verdict`, `adapter.py`, `assemble_diff_input.schema.json`, and `code-review.yaml` as edit targets requiring a new `commit_message` (or `head` ref for `git log`) parameter.
findings.finding: The commit message (which carries the `rebar-ticket:` trailer) is not part of the unified diff or `changed_files` and is not currently an input to `assemble_diff`. The plan says 'assemble_diff reads the trailer (all ids)' but the commit message must first be obtained (via `git log` or a new workflow input), and neither `produce_code_review_verdict`'s signature, `assemble_diff_input.schema.json`, the `code-review.yaml` inputs section, nor the `adapter.py` caller are listed as edit targets for this plumbing.
findings.location: What section (assemble.py trailer parsing) / src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py / src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly where and how the ticket-presence boolean is computed and injected into the overlay activation path (e.g., a new `ticket_triggered` output from assemble_diff, a new input to overlay_union, or a separate YAML step) so the `when:` wiring is fully defined.
findings.finding: The plan says the overlay fires 'only when a ticket resolves from the trailer' (a content/ticket-presence trigger), but never specifies the decision rule: where in the pipeline the `include_scope_intent` boolean is produced and how it flows into the YAML batch `when:` machinery. The existing `overlay_union` op computes booleans from glob + recommend only; no ticket-presence signal feeds it.
findings.location: What section (registry.py — 'content trigger'); Acceptance Criteria AC1; src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:74-96 (overlay_union); src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py` (`CodeReviewBatchRunner`) to the Scope as an edit target, since it must be modified to inject ticket context only for the `scope-intent` overlay.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py` (`CodeReviewBatchRunner`) is the sole mechanism that injects `ticket_context` into every overlay run (line 58: `inputs={'ticket_context': self._context, ...}`), but it is not named as an edit target. The plan requires gating ticket context exclusively to the `scope-intent` overlay — that requires modifying `CodeReviewBatchRunner` to support per-overlay context differentiation — but this file is absent from the Scope section.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism for per-overlay ticket-context isolation given that `CodeReviewBatchRunner` injects a single `ticket_context` to all criteria — e.g., whether scope-intent runs as a standalone step (not in the batch), or whether the batch runner gains a per-overlay context-override map.
findings.finding: The plan's ticket-context isolation contract ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others') conflicts with how CodeReviewBatchRunner actually injects context. CodeReviewBatchRunner is constructed with a single `context` string and injects `{'ticket_context': self._context, 'ticket_id': '(code review)'}` identically into every overlay's StepContext. There is no per-overlay context dispatch in the batch runner. Gating ticket context to scope-intent only while leaving all other overlays receiving only the diff context requires either (a) a new construction-time mechanism for per-overlay context overrides, (b) a separate non-batch step for scope-intent only, or (c) post-injection filtering in the overlay prompts — none of which are described by the plan.
findings.location: What section / batch_runner.py line 58 / code-review.yaml round_a/round_b batch steps

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the concrete isolation mechanism for ticket-context gating: specify whether `CodeReviewBatchRunner` is modified to accept a per-criterion context map, or whether `scope-intent` runs as a standalone step outside the batch (and update the Scope section to name the affected file).
findings.finding: The ticket-context isolation contract ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others') is an unverified capability assertion: `CodeReviewBatchRunner` (batch_runner.py:58) injects `{'ticket_context': self._context, 'ticket_id': ...}` uniformly to ALL included overlays via a single `_context` field. The existing batch-runner architecture has no per-criterion context-differentiation mechanism. The plan does not resolve how selective injection is achieved — e.g., whether it modifies the batch runner, adds a new `ticket_context_map` input, or runs scope-intent as a standalone step outside the batch entirely.
findings.location: What section — 'Ticket-blindness is NOT free' paragraph; assemble.py / batch_runner.py
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest approach with no batch-runner change: run `scope-intent` as a dedicated standalone step in `code-review.yaml` (like `base`) that receives `ticket_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.ticket_context }}` from a separate output key on `assemble_diff`, while the existing batch-runner steps continue to receive only the diff context. This naturally isolates ticket context without a batch runner change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the specific component (e.g., `CodeReviewBatchRunner`, a new per-overlay input map in the batch step, or a YAML `with:` conditional) responsible for routing ticket scope context exclusively to the `scope-intent` overlay, and specify it in the plan.
findings.finding: The ticket-context isolation mechanism ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others') has ambiguous placement. `CodeReviewBatchRunner` injects `ticket_context: self._context` into ALL overlay step inputs uniformly. The plan says 'explicit plumbing' is required but never specifies whether the branching lives in CodeReviewBatchRunner, assemble_diff outputs, YAML `with:` conditionals, or elsewhere.
findings.location: What section ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others'); src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:58; AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new `assemble_diff` output key that carries resolved ticket context (e.g. `ticket_scope_context`) and the corresponding `overlay_union` input key (e.g. `content_ticket_ids` or `tickets_resolved`) so the trigger contract and isolation boundary are unambiguous before implementation.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what new output key assemble_diff must emit to carry the commit message / resolved ticket ids downstream, nor does it name the new input assemble_diff_input schema field for reading the commit message — without this the overlay_union step has no signal to fire the content trigger.
findings.location: What section / assemble.py + assemble_diff_input schema
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'assemble_diff emits a new output key `ticket_scope_context` (None when no ticket resolves); overlay_union accepts a new optional `ticket_scope_context` input and sets `include_scope_intent: true` iff it is non-None; the gate YAML wires `steps.assemble_diff.outputs.ticket_scope_context` into overlay_union and into the scope-intent overlay step only.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope section as an edit target and include the `scope-intent` overlay bullet in the catalog.
findings.finding: `code-review-base.md` is not listed as an edit target, but adding `scope-intent` to `OVERLAY_IDS` will break the existing no-drift guard test (`test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`) which asserts the prompt's bullet catalog equals exactly `OVERLAY_IDS`. The test regex looks for `- \`<id>\` —` bullets; a new overlay id with no matching bullet → assertion failure.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope list and add a `- \`scope-intent\` — ...` catalog line to its overlay bullet list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py` (the `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` assertion) to the list of consumers requiring update when `scope-intent` is added to `OVERLAY_IDS`.
findings.finding: `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` hardcodes the exact 11-member set of `OVERLAY_IDS` and will fail when `scope-intent` is added. This test is an unenumerated consumer of `registry.py`.
findings.location: tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:20-33
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'scope-intent'` to the hardcoded set in `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` as an edit target and add a `scope-intent` entry with the required routing fields.
findings.finding: The code-review `criteria_routing.json` (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json`) is an unenumerated required edit target: `test_code_review_ws2.py:22` asserts `assert oid in idx` for every `OVERLAY_IDS` member, so `scope-intent` must have a routing entry or the WS2 test breaks. The plan's Scope section says 'criteria_routing.json (SHARED — coordinate with C, F)' but does not explicitly name the code-review routing file.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `code-review-base.md` to the Scope section and specify the `- \`scope-intent\` — …` catalog bullet that must be added to keep the no-drift test passing.
findings.finding: Adding `scope-intent` to OVERLAY_IDS will immediately break the existing no-drift guard test (test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids), which asserts code-review-base.md catalogs EXACTLY the OVERLAY_IDS set. The plan never mentions updating code-review-base.md's overlay catalog section.
findings.location: Scope section / What section (REGISTER scope-intent in OVERLAY_IDS); tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:148-156; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md (catalog bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope and include a `- \`scope-intent\` — diff scope / ticket-intent alignment.` bullet in the overlay catalog section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether ticket-scope data for `scope-intent` rides a new named YAML variable (no plumbing needed — other prompts won't reference it) or the engine-injected `ticket_context` slot (requires explicit suppression for all 15 existing `{{ticket_context}}` consumers); the current 'explicit plumbing' requirement is either YAGNI or under-specifies the mechanism depending on this choice.
findings.finding: The plan describes 'explicit plumbing (gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others)' as a required bespoke mechanism, but this framing is premature or ambiguous depending on how the ticket-scope data is delivered. Inspecting the codebase: all 11 existing overlay prompts, plus base/verify/coach, already contain `{{ticket_context}}` and receive it via `ticket_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }}` in the YAML (this slot carries the diff, not ticket scope). If the new scope data is delivered via a DISTINCT named YAML variable (e.g. a new `assemble_diff` output field `scope_context`), no suppression plumbing is needed — the other prompts simply won't reference a variable they don't declare. If it rides the existing `ticket_context` engine-injected slot, the leak is immediate and real across all 15 consumer prompts, and bespoke suppression machinery would be genuinely needed. The plan does not resolve which approach is intended, so AC3's 'explicit plumbing' requirement may either be YAGNI (if a new variable name suffices) or under-specifies the suppression mechanism (if the existing slot is co-opted).
findings.location: What section: 'Ticket-blindness is NOT free: ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input' / AC3: 'A test proves base + other overlays receive NO ticket context (ticket-blind)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use a new named assemble_diff output field (e.g. `scope_context`) wired only to the scope-intent overlay step — this isolates ticket scope without any suppression machinery, and AC3's test becomes a simple check that the other overlay prompts don't declare `scope_context` in their `with:` blocks.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the premise in the 'Ticket-blindness is NOT free' section: the live code-review.yaml already passes NO ticket_context to round_a/round_b overlays; restate the real plumbing task as introducing a NEW, distinct assemble_diff output key for ticket-scope context and wiring it exclusively to the scope-intent overlay step.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input, so keeping base + other overlays ticket-blind requires explicit plumbing (gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others)' — but the live YAML already does NOT pass any ticket_context to round_a or round_b overlay batch steps. The real risk is distinct: the new ticket-scope context assembled from the trailer must not be conflated with or override the existing `steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context` already wired as `ticket_context` to base/verify/coach. The 'explicit plumbing' concern is real but the stated premise (current overlays receive ticket_context and must be actively blinded) is factually incorrect per the live code-review.yaml.
findings.location: What section — 'Ticket-blindness is NOT free: ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input, so keeping base + other overlays ticket-blind requires explicit plumbing'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace: 'ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input, so keeping base + other overlays ticket-blind requires explicit plumbing' with: 'round_a/round_b overlays already receive no ticket_context; the new work is emitting a SEPARATE output key (e.g. ticket_scope_context) from assemble_diff and wiring it only to the scope-intent overlay step — not conflating it with the existing diff-context key that base/verify/coach consume.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and implement graceful degradation for unresolvable ticket IDs in assemble.py: specify whether the overlay fires on partial ticket resolution, logs a warning for unresolved IDs, and never propagates a lookup exception to the calling pipeline.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify error handling or graceful degradation when a ticket ID extracted from the rebar-ticket: trailer cannot be resolved to actual ticket data (e.g., missing file, unknown ID, API unavailable). The overlay is advisory, but silent failure vs. logged skip vs. hard error is unspecified.
findings.location: AC: 'assemble_diff reads the trailer (all ids) and assembles union scope context' / assemble.py ticket-context assembly

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the ticket-context isolation contract (why ticket context is gated to scope-intent only and excluded from base/other overlays) in CLAUDE.md, an ADR, or an inline design note in assemble.py, so future overlay authors understand the invariant.
findings.finding: The ticket-context isolation contract — gating ticket context exclusively to the scope-intent overlay while keeping base and all other overlays ticket-blind — is a novel architectural decision about reviewer_input partitioning that is not planned to be captured in any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc. Future overlay authors will have no explanation for why ticket context is unavailable to them.
findings.location: Scope section / assemble.py plumbing description
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to CLAUDE.md (or an inline block comment in assemble.py near the gating logic) explaining: 'ticket_context is intentionally scoped to the scope-intent overlay; do not pass it into shared reviewer_input — this preserves ticket-blindness for base review and all other overlays. See AC: ticket-blind test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior when ticket resolution partially or fully fails: e.g., 'overlay fires only if at least one ticket resolves; unresolvable IDs are silently skipped; if no tickets resolve, overlay is skipped with a coverage-gap note.'
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for partial or failed ticket resolution: when `extract_ticket_refs` returns multiple IDs but only some resolve (or none resolve), the plan is silent on whether the overlay fires with partial context, fires with empty context, or does not fire at all.
findings.location: What section ('assemble_diff reads the trailer (all ids) and assembles union scope context'); AC1 ('overlay fires only when a ticket resolves'); AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt the existing fail-open convention: assemble union context from whichever ticket IDs resolve; if none resolve, skip the overlay and emit a coverage-gap note (matching `base_failure_result` pattern in registry.py).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace '(eval)' in AC4 with a concrete proving artifact: either a unit test with a synthetic multi-ticket diff fixture asserting no scope-drift finding is emitted, or a deterministic snapshot/golden-output check, so the criterion is reproducibly verifiable in CI.
findings.finding: AC4 uses '(eval)' as its proving mechanism for the multi-ticket no-false-drift claim, but 'eval' is not a deterministic, in-session-completable check — it implies a manual or subjective LLM-output judgment with no reproducible pass/fail criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'A multi-ticket commit naming all its tickets produces no false scope-drift finding (eval).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pytest fixture that constructs a two-ticket commit (rebar-ticket: T-1 T-2) where the diff is wholly within both tickets' stated scope, runs the scope-intent overlay, and asserts zero findings are returned. Reference this test file in AC4.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC/test for an edge/failure path: e.g., one referenced ticket fails to resolve (overlay falls back gracefully), a malformed/empty trailer (overlay does not fire or errors cleanly), or a true-positive scenario where a diff clearly escapes ticket scope and the overlay produces a finding — to verify the overlay functions correctly beyond the no-false-positive case.
findings.finding: AC4 provides only a happy-path eval (multi-ticket commit with all tickets present produces no false positive). No criterion or test addresses failure/edge paths for the overlay: e.g., one referenced ticket fails to resolve (partial context), an empty or malformed rebar-ticket: trailer, or a diff that clearly escapes all referenced ticket scopes (true-positive detection). The overlay is user-facing (it surfaces advisory findings to code reviewers), so some coverage of these paths is warranted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'When the trailer references a ticket that cannot be resolved, the overlay either omits that ticket's context with a logged warning or does not fire — verified by a unit test.' And add an eval scenario: 'A diff that modifies code unrelated to any referenced ticket produces at least one scope-drift finding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document the explicit contract for assemble_diff when no rebar-ticket: trailer is present (e.g., ticket_context key is absent vs. empty list), and confirm existing callers are tolerant of the new key.
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding trailer parsing to assemble_diff but does not explicitly state the no-trailer backward-compatible path: what ticket_context value (absent key, empty list, None) assemble_diff produces when the commit has no rebar-ticket: trailer. Existing callers of assemble_diff that lack a trailer could receive an unexpected new key in reviewer_input unless the contract is defined.
findings.location: ## Scope / assemble.py section; AC item 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to the assemble.py description: 'When no rebar-ticket: trailer is found, ticket_context is omitted from reviewer_input entirely (not set to None or []); the scope-intent overlay's content trigger checks for key presence.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale explaining why trailer parsing and ticket-context assembly belongs in `assemble_diff` (e.g., 'single assembly point, DiffContext carries all context') versus the `gate_dispatch` orchestration layer.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why trailer parsing belongs specifically inside assemble_diff / assemble.py rather than at the gate_dispatch layer (produce_code_review_verdict), which already has access to head, base, and repo_root and is the natural integration point for commit-level metadata.
findings.location: What section / Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit proving commands or test references for AC1 (e.g., a test asserting the overlay is skipped when no rebar-ticket: trailer is present, and fires when one is) and AC5 (e.g., a test or config-inspection assertion that the overlay's output is marked advisory/non-blocking).
findings.finding: ACs 1 and 5 have no named test or proving command: AC1 ('overlay fires only when a ticket resolves from the trailer') and AC5 ('Overlay is advisory') each assert a behavioral property but the plan specifies no mechanism to verify them — neither a test nor an inspection command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 and AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the `criteria_routing.json` entry for `scope-intent` is present and correctly configured (e.g., routing to the appropriate consumer, consistent with the C/F coordination note).
findings.finding: The Scope section names `criteria_routing.json` as a deliverable ('SHARED — coordinate with C, F'), but no AC covers or verifies the routing changes made to that file.
findings.location: Scope section — `criteria_routing.json (SHARED — coordinate with C, F)`
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] `criteria_routing.json` contains a `scope-intent` entry consistent with the routing agreed with tracks C and F.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out step for the assemble.py ticket-context threading change (e.g., a feature flag that disables trailer parsing, or an expand-contract note explaining how to revert to ticket-blind behavior).
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges that ticket-context leaking into existing overlays is a risk and requires a test (AC3), but provides no explicit rollback or back-out path if the gating is defective and ships to production.
findings.location: What section — 'Ticket-blindness is NOT free … gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others. Verify with a test.' and AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new `assemble_diff` output key for ticket scope/AC context (e.g., `ticket_scope_context`), add it to `assemble_diff_output.schema.json`, and include that schema file in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The `assemble_diff_output` schema (assemble_diff_output.schema.json) currently only declares `context` and `changed_files`. The plan requires assemble_diff to emit assembled ticket scope/AC context, but never names the new output key, its type, or that the schema must be extended — leaving the downstream YAML wiring undefined.
findings.location: What section (assemble_diff — 'assemble the referenced tickets' scope/AC as this overlay's context'); src/rebar/schemas/assemble_diff_output.schema.json; Acceptance Criteria AC2

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
